title: eupatorium cannabinum subsp. syriacum
intro: Eupatorium cannabinum subsp. syriacum, also known as Syrian hemp agrimony, is a subspecies of the eupatorium cannabinum plant, a member of the asteraceae family. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Syrian hemp agrimony include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and promote healing, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Syrian hemp agrimony can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to respiratory infections.
